1	# Proposals for issue 14268
2	
3	## Proposal 0
4	
5	# Proposal
6	
7	## Problem 
8	As the description says we don't count the actual sending characters, instead we just count the typed characters to determine a valid length.
9	
10	## Solution 1
11	
12	Count the html parsed characters and limit it to 60K as that is the limit that has been set in the backend/DB.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Proposal 1
17	
18	## Proposal
19	
20	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
21	
22	The composer does not prevent sending comments containing large amounts of markdown that result in a `402 Maximum Size Exceeded` error from the API endpoints.
23	
24	### What is the root cause of that problem?
25	
26	The comment length calculation does not account for the markdown conversion.
27	
28	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
29	
30	I propose three changes to solve this issue:
31	1. Include the markdown transformation in the `getCommentLength` function. 
32	2. Defer counting until there is a break in typing, since it's too expensive to perform for each key-press.
33	3. Abstract the calculation to a pure component, so calculating is only occurring once each time.
34	
35	A demonstration of the deferred counter can be viewed in the following video.
36	
37	The counter is normally hidden until the limit is reached, but was enabled permanently for this example:
38	
39	[Screencast from 2023-02-10 02:23:47 AM.webm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1311325/218070115-a5b19680-72af-402b-bf0e-2a0dfdcf7d3a.webm)
40	
41	By adopting these changes, we are increasing the performance of the composer beyond where it's currently at, while at the same time solving this issue.  The app is currently counting comment length four times per keypress due to re-renders of the composer components.
42	
43	See also [this screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1311325/218080532-ea6371b6-54b5-4355-ad7f-1b5eccbea726.png) demonstrating the accurate character count for the OP of this issue.
44	
45	### Additional clarifications from the same user in later comments
46	
47	
48	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1430846150
49	
50	> It actually increases typing performance for long messages. We're currently encoding the message four times for every keypress. By  debouncing the calculation until there is a 500ms break in typing, we are eliminating any process blocking while typing characters.
51	>
52	> The plain-text message will still contain the markdown syntax. The user would have no indication why their message was not formatted as expected. In a professional context this could reflect poorly on the sender of the message.
53	>
54	> My solution also takes into account the 10,000 character plain-text conversion by abstracting the same function.  No markdown conversions would be happening in the counter beyond this amount either.
55	>
56	> I disagree. The addition of a server limit constant means it would need to be updated along with other code changes. It's a big part of the reason why we did not want to add such a constant for the other issue.  The reality is that updating the value will be overlooked and this issue will re-appear.
57	
58	
59	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1430873635
60	
61	> We are not actually modifying what they're sending, we're infact sending the text as it is if it's greater than 10K.
62	>
63	> It's better to let users control what they are sending rather than modifying their message without their knowledge.
64	>
65	> The conversion to plain-text at 10,000 characters is because the server has trouble processing that amount of markdown, and a workaround like that should be avoided if possible.
66	>
67	> I disagree. The addition of a server limit constant means it would need to be updated along with other code changes.
68	
69	
70	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1432848013
71	
72	> Thank you @francoislm
73	>
74	> I can demonstrate the performance enhancement with two videos.
75	>
76	> As you can see, the test above completely locks up my browser while performing key events due to the thousands of times the `getCommentLength` function is being invoked.  The composer has noticeable lag, even with regular typing.
77	>
78	> In the second video, we see that the optimization greatly improves performance.
79	
80	
81	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1435015513
82	
83	> It was already decided to [encode the comment](https://github.com/redstar504/ExpensifyApp/blob/3850a7c219328ea0ba1c9f9422ef8591ea992cae/src/libs/ReportUtils.js#L1391) to determine the comment length in the other issue.
84	>
85	> I will report back with some benchmarks so we can get a better idea of how long the markdown conversion will take with the debouncing optimization.
86	
87	
88	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1435294395
89	
90	> I benchmarked the string from OP at around 250ms.  It was barely noticeable while adding or deleting text as you can see in the video below.  You would need to have an unrealistic amount of markdown and time it just right to notice it.
91	>
92	> [output.webm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1311325/219794385-0413b005-6a99-473e-92cc-9b3407c2bf8c.webm)
93	
94	
95	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1435355054
96	
97	> Parsing such an extreme amount of Markdown from OP is indeed slower on Android. It's not really a problem because it does not block the thread. I can continue typing while the calculation happens, as you can see.
98	>
99	> [Screencast from 2023-02-17 02:30:07 PM.webm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1311325/219807978-5d87cb61-c7b7-4cbc-87d6-4fa501113e28.webm)
100	
101	
102	#### Comment at https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/14268#issuecomment-1435388402
103	
104	> If we look at a string of `*b*` that would be allowed, there is no perceptible delay apart from the interval.
105	>
106	> [Screencast from 2023-02-17 03:17:37 PM.webm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1311325/219815360-7deb5850-d22c-4cc4-80e6-f80cabdd1b00.webm)

110	## Proposal 2
111	
112	# Proposal
113	
114	## Solution 2
115	
116	Send the non-parsed message if the parsed html message exceeds the 60K character limit as that is the limit that has been set in the backend/DB. Also, currently we don't parse text to MD/HTML when the text is above 10K characters so this can be a good enough solution.
117	
118	```diff
119	diff --git a/src/libs/ReportUtils.js b/src/libs/ReportUtils.js
120	index 45b9319a4..706846268 100644
121	--- a/src/libs/ReportUtils.js
122	+++ b/src/libs/ReportUtils.js
123	@@ -651,16 +651,17 @@ function buildOptimisticReportAction(sequenceNumber, text, file) {
124	     // For longer comments, skip parsing and display plaintext for performance reasons. It takes over 40s to parse a 100k long string!!
125	     const parser = new ExpensiMark();
126	     const commentText = text.length < 10000 ? parser.replace(text) : text;
127	+    const finalCommentText = commentText < 60000 ? commentText : text;
128	     const isAttachment = _.isEmpty(text) && file !== undefined;
129	     const attachmentInfo = isAttachment ? file : {};
130	-    const htmlForNewComment = isAttachment ? 'Uploading Attachment...' : commentText;
131	+    const htmlForNewComment = isAttachment ? 'Uploading Attachment...' : finalCommentText;
132	
133	     // Remove HTML from text when applying optimistic offline comment
134	     const textForNewComment = isAttachment ? '[Attachment]'
135	         : parser.htmlToText(htmlForNewComment);
136	
137	     return {
138	-        commentText,
139	+        commentText: finalCommentText,
140	         reportAction: {
141	             reportActionID: NumberUtils.rand64(),
142	             actionName: CONST.REPORT.ACTIONS.TYPE.ADDCOMMENT,
143	```
144	
145	---
146	
147	We should check the length of the HTML text before sending, and if it is greater than the message character limit then we should just send the plain text instead of the HTML text. (We do this already for text greater than 10K characters)
148	
149	@aimane-chnaif
150	
151	---
152	
153	## Proposal 3
154	
155	## Proposal
156	
157	The optimization was briefly explored in my recent [PR](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/14752) for counting the proper length of non-ASCII/emoji characters. I was only able to achieve it using a hook-based approach, so the change was deferred.
158	
159	Since then I have refactored it into a very simple class-based [implementation](https://github.com/redstar504/ExpensifyApp/pull/12/files).
160	
161	## Final Notes
162	
163	Consider the example of a user composing a rich message consisting of a several emojis, bilingual characters, and formatted markdown text. This comment will add up to the limit very quickly. If we don't notify the user that the message is too long before sending it, they will have no clue why it's being converted to plain text.
164	
165	The message would be full of raw markdown syntax that would likely confuse the other participant. It's better from a UX perspective to let them know it's too long and give them the opportunity to revise it before sending.
166	
167	---

1 # Proposals for issue 15193 2 3 ## Proposal 0 4 5 ## Proposal 6 7 ### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue. 8 Bold text appears temporarily, then reverts to normal text when sending `# ```to send``` ` message 9 10 ### What is the root cause of the problem? 11 When the message is synched with the backend it's being changed from the original one: 12 - original message: ` # bold code ` 13 - The message gets HTML-escaped before synching with the backend: ` 

bold code
 `
14	- Then message is send to the backend, and after being processed on the backend it looks as following: ` 
bold code
 `
15	
16	Therefore the text flickering (from bold to regular) happens due to this change on the backend.
17	This can be clearly seen in offline mode: the message stays bold forever, until the app goes back online
18	
19	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
20	To make sure everything is consistent I suggest to simply get rid of Bold property in the message before it's even rendered for the first time.
21	The change can be made [in this method](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/pages/home/report/ReportActionCompose.js#L471)
22	The advantage of this solution is the fact that it's going to be very clean, we won't need to make changes on the backend, and we'll maintain consistency w/o introducing any regressions...
23	
24	---
25	
26	## Proposal 1
27	
28	## Proposal
29	
30	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
31	Message turns bold temporarily when enclosed with a `#` followed by ``` or `.
32	
33	### What is the root cause of that problem?
34	The frontend parses the expression in a different way then the backend. Frontend encloses the `
` tag inside the `h1` tag while the backend does not enclose the `pre` tag inside `h1`.
35	
36	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
37	
38	We can change the ExpensiMark `#` parsing rule such that it ignores adding `h1` if `pre` or `code` tag is present.
39	The rule exists here:
40	https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L166
41	
42	The new regex would look like the following:
43	```
44	/^#(?!#) +(?!<(pre|code)>)(.+)$\s*/gm
45	```
46	
47	The [replacement part](https://github.com/Expensify/expensify-common/blob/4f61e5529c89936bfb72b6cee53250990c16f188/lib/ExpensiMark.js#L167) of this regex needs to be changed as well:
48	
49	```
50	replacement: '
$2
',
51	```
52	
53	This works well after the fix.
54	
55	https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30054992/219319194-71f8e132-3e57-4cc9-8d5e-91fd9323352f.mov
56	
57	---
58	
59	## Proposal 2
60	
61	## Proposal (from comment #1432985056 by moezh)
62	
63	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
64	Bold text appears temporarily, then reverts to normal text, when sending ``# ```code``` ``.
65	
66	### What is the root cause of that problem?
67	The problem is related to the way the frontend and backend parse expressions, resulting in differences between the two. Specifically, the frontend sends `
code
`, while the backend replies with `
code
`.
68	
69	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
70	To address the root cause of the problem, I recommend making the following changes:
71	
72	- Adjust the CSS for the "pre" class to prevent it from inheriting font weight and size from its parent.
73	- Change the CSS for the "h1" class to use lineHeight instead of marginBottom, to take into consideration that the title might be empty.
74	
75	I believe that the changes outlined above are the most effective and simple way to address the problem.
76	
77	https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2527288/219359884-b7ea7b53-0c85-4e17-8d56-9f55c2e6a86a.mov
78	
79	### What alternative solutions did you explore? (Optional)
80	None
81	
82	--- Additional clarifications from comment #1441316394 (also by moezh) ---
83	
84	"Here's a more detailed explanation of the changes i'll make to the CSS (src/styles/styles.js) for the "pre" and "h1" classes, in order to solve the problem:
85	
86	For the "pre" class, I'll add following properties:
87	- Add fontWeight, fontSize, and lineHeight: This ensures that the "pre" element doesn't inherit these properties from its parent, and instead uses the default properties.
88	
89	For the "h1" class, I'll change following properties:
90	- Remove marginTop and marginBottom:  This ensures that there is no extra space above and below the element.
91	- Add lineHeight: This property replaces marginTop and marginBottom properties that were removed.
92	
93	These changes will ensure that the "pre" element doesn't inherit styles from its parent, and that the "h1" element has consistent line height regardless of the content inside the element (empty or not). The frontend markup `
code
` and backend markup `
code
` will look identical in terms of styling."
94	
95	---

105	# ```test```
106	```
107	will be displayed in bold and later be normal.

110	Frontend translates `# ```test```` to `
test

test

113	To fix this issue, we need to check if the heading contains `
` code fence, then split or adjust accordingly so the heading is empty once code fence is detected, something like:

119	  if no '

' => return '# ' + g1 + g2
121	  if heading doesn't start with

122	},
123	```
124

test
`.

138	## Proposal 4
139	
140	## Proposal
141	
142	### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue.
143	Various text styles (bold, italic, etc) are applied to code block
144	
145	### What is the root cause of that problem?
146	When code block is inside formatting markdown (i.e. # or *), it is treated as normal text and text styles are applied.
147	Both frontend and backend issues are here.
148	i.e.
149	````*Test1 ```code``` Test2*````
150	In frontend, this is converted to `Test1 
code
 Test2` (good)
151	But in backend, it is converted to `Test1 
code
 Test2` (bad)
152	
153	````# Test1 ```code``` Test2````
154	In frontend, this is converted to `
Test1 
code
 Test2
` (bad)
155	But in backend, it is converted to `
Test1 
code
 Test2` (good)
156	
157	### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem?
158	We can override formatted text style in code block with code block text style.
159	https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/a1ca41521c69fa1caf8e0351ef62292238379d63/src/components/HTMLEngineProvider/BaseHTMLEngineProvider.js#L31-L47
160	To achieve this, add custom `pre` styles in customHTMLElementModels of engine provider
161	```
162	    pre: defaultHTMLElementModels.div.extend({
163	        tagName: 'pre',
164	        mixedUAStyles: {fontStyle: 'normal', fontWeight: 'normal'},
165	    }),
166	```
167	
168	`fontStyle: 'normal', fontWeight: 'normal'` - overrides italic, bold styles
169	
170	Other markdowns work well but `h1` needs update a bit to prevent applying h1 style to text after code block on the same line:
171	```diff
172	            {
173	                name: 'heading1',
174	-               regex: /^#(?!#) +(?! )(.+)$\s*/gm,
175	+               regex: /^# +(?! )((?:(?!
).)+)\s*/gm,
176	                replacement: '
$1
',
177	            },
178	```
179	Regex explanation:
180	i.e. ````# Test1 ```code``` Test2````
181	Currently, h1 style is applied to all including code block and `Test2` but as per the expected behavior, h1 style should be applied to `Test1` only.
182	So if string includes `
`, stop at here and wrap with h1 tag.
183	Optional: `(?!#)` is redundant because next character requires space. So this is just for optimization.
184	
185	As explained in root cause section, bold issue should be fixed in backend as well.
186	
187	**After fix:**
188	
189	````# Test1 ```code``` Test2````
190	In frontend, this is converted to `
Test1 
code
 Test2` (to fix)
191	No change requires in backend
192	
193	````*Test1 ```code``` Test2*````
194	In frontend, this is converted to `Test1 
code
 Test2` (no change)
195	In backend, keep frontend html, not changing to `Test1 
code
 Test2` (to fix).
196	Same applies to italic (`_`, ``)
197	
198	Demo:
199	
200	https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/108292595/224970806-69c02823-99fd-49e8-8dbb-ae9ad15aa635.mov
201	
202	All work well in offline mode. But when online, bold and italic still applied to code block until backend fix.
203	
204	---
205	
206	## Proposal 5
207	
208	Ignoring the `#` if any tag is present after it. I think we don't want to do that. We should ignore only if `code` or `pre` tag is present.
209	
210	---

189	Here are the steps we should take to implement this feature:
190	
191	- Add the `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` button to `Header with back button` component [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/f48a2085d2e22bc011c4028fce56413d088b7896/src/pages/iou/WaypointEditor.js#L116), this will ensure we have the three dots menu.
192	- Add a state variable on the page [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/f48a2085d2e22bc011c4028fce56413d088b7896/src/pages/iou/WaypointEditor.js#L60) to track if the modal is open. we will name it `isModalVisible` and set the default value false.
193	  ```js
194	  const [isDeleteModalVisible, setIsDeleteModalVisible] = useState(false);
195	  ```
196	- Add the custom menu item to a const variable:
197		```js
198		const threeDotsMenuItems = {
199	        icon: Expensicons.Trashcan,
200	        translationKey: 'common.delete',
201	        action: () => setIsDeleteModalVisible(true)
202		}
203		```
204		This has the common delete text and pressing it sets the Delete Modal Visibility to `true`. We will now pass this as a prop to HeaderWithBackButton:
205		```js
206		<HeaderWithBackButton
207		      threeDotsMenuItems={threeDotsMenuItems}
208		      ...
209		/>
210		```
211	- Add the `ConfirmModal` component to the code [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/f48a2085d2e22bc011c4028fce56413d088b7896/src/pages/iou/WaypointEditor.js#L155) and make sure it is visible only while the variable we defined has the `true` value.
212		```js
213		{isDeleteModalVisible &&
214			<ConfirmModal
215	    		title={props.translate('waypoint.common.delete')}
216	    		isVisible={isDeleteModalOpen}
217	    		onConfirm={deleteWaypointAndHideModal}
218	    		onCancel={() => setIsDeleteModalVisible(false)}
219	    		prompt={props.translate('waypoint.common.deleteConfirmation')}
220	    		confirmText={props.translate('common.delete')}
221	    		cancelText={props.translate('common.cancel')}
222	    		danger
223			/>
224		}
225		```
226	
227	- Now we will be defining the `deleteWaypointAndHideModal` function which will be using the `removeWaypoint` function from [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/252cc247dbeed5b7e4b6cbaa15f91a2bdb20b520/src/libs/actions/Transaction.js#L92) (passing it the `transactionID from props` and the `waypointIndex` from the route params) and will hide the modal using `setIsDeleteModalVisible(false)`
228	
229	-  We will be adding the translations [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/0b7ae7f58e3a98ba49cf9fc2812921964d9d8895/src/languages/en.js#L6) and [here](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/0b7ae7f58e3a98ba49cf9fc2812921964d9d8895/src/languages/es.js#L5) under a `waypoint.common:` key
230	
231	### What alternative solutions did you explore? (Optional)
232	xx

text:
Let me analyze each task carefully, reviewing the issue and all proposals to make a maintainer-quality decision. ## Task 14268: Message character counting issue **Issue:** When sending/editing a message with `*a*` repeated 3333 times (or similar markdown that expands significantly when parsed to HTML), the API request fails with "402 Maximum Size Exceeded" because the character count is calculated on the typed text rather than the final HTML-converted text. **Proposals:** - **Proposal 0:** (Very brief) Count HTML parsed characters and limit to 60K (backend limit). No details on implementation. - **Proposal 1:** Three changes: 1. Include markdown transformation in `getCommentLength` 2. Defer counting until a break in typing (debounce) for performance 3. Abstract calculation to a pure component to avoid re-renders This is a thorough approach with performance considerations, benchmarks, and actual testing. - **Proposal 2:** Send the non-parsed (plain) message if the parsed HTML exceeds 60K limit. Essentially, if HTML > 60K, fall back to sending plain text. This is a workaround rather than a proper fix — it silently changes what gets sent without informing the user. It also doesn't prevent the user from hitting the limit, just silently degrades their message. - **Proposal 3:** A class-based implementation for counting comment length properly, building on prior work from another PR. Focused on accurate counting. **Analysis:** - Proposal 2 is the weakest — it silently changes what the user sends (falls back to plain text) without notification. This is bad UX. - Proposal 0 is too brief to evaluate seriously. - Proposal 1 is the most thorough — it correctly identifies the root cause (counting typed chars instead of HTML), provides performance optimizations (debouncing to avoid blocking the UI on every keystroke), and properly prevents sending messages that exceed the backend limit. The author benchmarked the performance and addressed concerns about mobile performance. - Proposal 3 is reasonable but less comprehensive than Proposal 1. **Decision:** Proposal 1 should be accepted. It correctly identifies the root cause, proposes a complete solution with performance considerations, and was thoroughly tested. ## Task 15193: Code block appears bold temporarily with header formatting **Issue:** When starting a message with `#` then a space, then adding a code block (e.g., `# ```code``` `), the text appears bold temporarily and then reverts to normal. The flicker happens because frontend parses it as `

code

` (making it all bold under h1), while the backend returns `

code
` (with empty h1 and code block separately). **Proposals:** - **Proposal 0:** Strip the bold property from the message before rendering. This is a cosmetic workaround that doesn't fix the actual parsing inconsistency. - **Proposal 1:** Modify the ExpensiMark `#` parsing regex to ignore adding `h1` if a `
` or `` tag follows. This fixes the frontend parsing to match the backend's behavior. The regex `/^#(?!#) +(?!<(pre|code)>)(.+)$\s*/gm` is clean and precise.

- **Proposal 2:** CSS-only fix — make `
` not inherit font-weight and font-size from parent, and adjust h1 lineHeight. This is a cosmetic band-aid that doesn't fix the markup mismatch. The underlying HTML still differs between frontend and backend.

- **Proposal 3:** Fix the heading regex/replacement to account for `
` inside heading content. Similar approach to Proposal 1 but uses a function-based replacement instead of a regex change. Slightly more complex but achieves the same goal.

- **Proposal 4:** Combination approach — CSS for pre to override bold/italic, plus regex fix for h1. Also notes that bold/italic with code blocks need backend fixes too. This is more comprehensive but also more complex.

- **Proposal 5:** (Very brief) Argues that the fix should only ignore `#` if `code` or `pre` tags follow, rather than ignoring for any tag. This is more of a comment on Proposal 1/3 than a full proposal.

**Analysis:**
- Proposal 0 is cosmetic — doesn't fix root cause.
- Proposal 2 (CSS-only) is a band-aid. The HTML markup is still wrong; it just hides the visual issue.
- Proposal 1 is the best: it fixes the root cause by making the frontend parsing match the backend. The regex change is minimal, targeted, and has no side effects. It prevents h1 from being applied when code block content follows.
- Proposal 3 is similar to 1 but uses a function-based approach instead of regex. There's an advantage to keeping it simple — Proposal 1's regex is elegant.
- Proposal 4 is more comprehensive but also includes backend changes that are out of scope for a frontend PR.

**Decision:** Proposal 1 should be accepted. It fixes the root cause at the parsing level with a minimal, precise regex change, ensuring frontend and backend produce consistent markup.

## Task 25901: Add waypoint deletion UI

**Issue:** A feature request to add a clear UI for deleting waypoints on a distance/waypoint editor. Currently, the only way to delete a waypoint is to save it with an empty text input, which is not discoverable.

**Proposals:**

- **Proposal 0:** Two approaches:
  1. Three-dot menu in the waypoint editor → "Remove Stop" → confirmation modal
  2. A (-) minus icon on the waypoint list (instead of DotIndicator) for direct deletion
  Also suggests creating a common delete modal component.

- **Proposal 1:** Adds `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` prop to `HeaderWithBackButton` in WaypointEditor, with a trashcan icon and delete option. Uses ConfirmModal.

- **Proposal 2:** Similar approach — three-dot menu with "Delete Waypoint" option, ConfirmModal, calls `removeWaypoint` from TransactionUtils. Includes withWindowDimensions HOC for proper positioning.

- **Proposal 3:** Very detailed — passes `shouldShowThreeDotsButton` and `threeDotsMenuItems` to HeaderWithBackButton, uses ConfirmModal with translation keys for "delete stop" text. Calls `Transaction.removeWaypoint` with transactionID and waypointIndex. Complete working video shown.

- **Proposal 4:** Similar to Proposal 1/3 — shows adding three-dots to HeaderWithBackButton, ConfirmModal, calls `removeWaypoint`. Includes translation additions.

**Analysis:**
- All proposals essentially suggest the same approach: three-dot menu → delete option → confirmation modal.
- Proposal 0 goes a step further by also suggesting deletion from the waypoint list (not just inside the editor). It's the most comprehensive in terms of UX, but may be over-engineering.
- Proposals 1, 2, 3, and 4 are very similar — three-dot button on the header, confirm modal, call removeWaypoint.
- Proposal 3 is the most detailed with specific code examples, translation keys, and a complete working video. The use of `translate('common.delete')`, `translate('distance.deleteStop')`, etc. shows attention to i18n.
- Proposal 1 is a bit less detailed.
- Proposal 4 has some typos in the code (e.g., `isModalVisible` vs `isDeleteModalOpen` inconsistency).

**Decision:** Proposal 3 should be accepted. It's the most complete and well-thought-out — references exact translation keys, shows the full implementation including the ConfirmModal props, and provides a complete working video. The approach matches what Proposal 0 describes but with more concrete detail. The single approach (header three-dot menu → confirmation modal) is focused and doesn't over-engineer.

Now let me write the decisions to manager_decisions.json.

I've read all three issues and all their proposals. Let me now make my maintainer-quality decisions and write the output file.

**Summary of my reasoning:**

### Issue 14268 — Message character counting fails with markdown expansion
- **Proposal 0** is too brief to evaluate.
- **Proposal 2** silently falls back to plain text when HTML exceeds the limit — bad UX, hides the issue from the user.
- **Proposal 3** is reasonable but less comprehensive.
- **Proposal 1** wins: it correctly identifies the root cause (counting typed chars instead of HTML output), includes debouncing for performance (benchmarked at ~250ms for extreme cases, offloaded from the render path), and properly prevents sending overly long messages.

### Issue 15193 — Code block flashes bold under header formatting
- **Proposals 0 & 2** are cosmetic band-aids (strip bold, or CSS-only) that don't fix the underlying parsing mismatch.
- **Proposal 1** wins: a minimal, precise regex change (`/^#(?!#) +(?!<(pre|code)>)(.+)$\s*/gm`) to the ExpensiMark parser that makes the frontend match the backend's parsing behavior. Fixes root cause with no side effects.
- **Proposal 3** is similar but more complex; **Proposal 4** mixes in backend changes that are out of scope.

### Issue 25901 — Add waypoint deletion UI
- All proposals converge on the same pattern: three-dot menu → delete option → confirmation modal → `removeWaypoint`.
- **Proposal 3** wins: the most detailed and complete — references exact translation keys, shows full ConfirmModal prop setup, and includes a working solution video. Single focused approach without over-engineering.

Done. `manager_decisions.json` has been written to the repo root with decisions for all three tasks. Here's the summary:

| Issue | Selected Proposal | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **14268** | **1** | Correctly counts HTML-converted length (not raw text), adds debouncing for performance, and prevents backend rejection — not a silent fallback like Proposal 2. |
| **15193** | **1** | Fixes the root cause at the parser level with a minimal regex change (`/^#(?!#) +(?!<(pre|code)>)(.+)$\s*/gm`) so frontend and backend markup match. Not a cosmetic CSS band-aid. |
| **25901** | **3** | Most detailed and complete implementation of the three-dot menu → confirm modal → `removeWaypoint` pattern, with proper translation keys and a working demo. Single focused scope. |
